question_asks: What is the average annual private medical insurance (PMI) premium for each level of coverage?
anchor_measures: No published avg PMI premium £ figure in these sources; leave estimate-flag

question_asks: What criteria are used to determine eligibility for a business need company car?

Does your organisation provide company cars as a status or grade-based benefit?
anchor_measures: Company car is predominantly a status/seniority benefit: ~37% of UK employers offer it (2% to all staff, 35% dependent on grade/seniority/role). Car allowance more common: ~47% (5% all, 42% dependent). All-employee car-ownership scheme rare (~8%).
source: CIPD "Reward Management Survey 2022: Focus on employee benefits" (April 2022, ref tcm18-10

question_asks: Are employees eligible to choose between a status-based company car and a cash allowance?

anchor_measures: Benefits review: 26% regularly review benefits package to ensure fair; 83% with objectives review

question_asks: Do benefits or health plans include support that is explicitly trans-inclusive (where applicable/legally permitted)?
Does the organisation track enrolment and utilisation for key benefits (e.g., pension, private medical, wellbeing benefits)?
What was employer pension contribution as a typical percentage of pensionable pay for the main population?
anchor_measures: Employer 5-8% typical; 3% legal min
source: CIPD/Pensions Regulator 2025/26

question_asks: What proportion of employees actively enrol in at least one optional/flexible benefit (excluding statutory benefits)?

Do you offer relocation support for hires who need to move location for their role?
What was the average redundancy cost per employee in the last completed 12-month period?
anchor_measures: Statutory redundancy reference (April 2026): weekly-pay cap £751, max statutory payment £22,530 (20yrs × 1.5wk × £751). Formula: 0.5wk/yr under-22, 1wk/yr 22-40, 1.5wk/yr 41+; max 20 yrs counted. Eligibility: 2+ years service.
source: gov.uk / Acas / business.gov.uk + Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order 2026; Emplo

question_asks: How is notice typically handled in redundancy situations?
anchor_measures: Statutory minimum notice (employer to employee): 1 week if employed 1 month-2 years; then 1 week per complete year, capped at 12 weeks (12+ years). Employee to employer: 1 week minimum after 1 month. Redundancy notice follows the same statutory floor.
source: gov.uk / Acas / business.gov.uk + Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order 2026; Emplo

question_asks: What pay basis is typically used to calculate redundancy payments?
Is there a documented redundancy process applied consistently?
anchor_measures: Documented redundancy process: best-practice, no clean prevalence %
source: CIPD redundancy guidance 2026

question_asks: Are selection criteria for redundancy documented and objective?

anchor_measures: Enhanced redundancy: common in large/structured employers; no single clean %
source: CIPD redundancy 2026; IRIS 2025/26

question_asks: What is the maximum redundancy pay multiple offered, excluding notice?
anchor_measures: Statutory cap £719/wk 2025/26 (£751 Apr 2026); max 30 weeks (20yr x1.5)
source: IRIS / theemploymentlawsolicitors 2026

anchor_measures: PMI PREVALENCE corrected to CIPD 2026 Grade A all-UK: 18% of all UK employers offer PMI/health insurance (70% of large 250+ employers; 17-18% of SMEs). Of large employers offering PMI, only ~50% extend it to all staff. Excess/cost-share design detail: Broadstone 2023 (Grade B large-skew) - 53% of PMI schemes on zero or £100 excess, £100 long the mainstay; claims inflation ~18%/yr driving shift to higher excess.

question_asks: Does your PMI include mental health cover and/or digital GP access?
anchor_measures: Digital/24-7 mental-health support is now mainstream alongside PMI: 52% of all UK employers offer 24/7 access to mental-health support/counselling (for all employees); 34% offer 24/7 virtual GP access; 89% of wellbeing activity includes a mental-health focus. PMI increasingly bundles MH cover + digital GP. (CIPD H&W 2025 all-UK, A-grade.)

question_asks: Is paid study time provided for employees undertaking funded qualifications?

Sits within a strong L&D set: 92% offer training/career-development, 75% pay professional subscriptions/fees. NOTE: 70% measures paid study LEAVE; the proportion offering protected paid study TIME (day-release) specifically may be somewhat lower - treat as upper bound.

question_asks: Which role levels are eligible for a status car allowance or lease car?

anchor_measures: Statutory employer-to-employee notice floor: 1 wk (1mth-2yr service), +1 wk per year thereafter, max 12 wks. Contractual notice by level sits above this.
source: gov.uk / Acas / business.gov.uk + Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order 2026; Emplo

question_asks: What is the employee notice period by level?
anchor_measures: Statutory employee-to-employer notice floor: 1 week minimum once employed 1 month+. Contractual employee notice by level sits above this.
source: gov.uk / Acas / business.gov.uk + Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order 2026; Emplo

question_asks: What is the annual car allowance amount by level?

question_asks: Do you offer enhanced carer's leave (paid) above the statutory unpaid entitlement?
anchor_measures: Paid carer's leave (above the statutory 1-week UNPAID entitlement) offered by ~31-45% of UK employers as a support form (45% of those offering carer support cite paid leave; ~36% paid carer's leave overall per RMS 2022). Above-statutory practice - the Carer's Leave Act 2023 floor is UNPAID.
source: CIPD "Flexible and hybrid working practices in 2025" (July 2025, ref 8909). YouGov, n=2,05

question_asks: Have you made paternity and unpaid parental leave available from day one of employment (ERA 2025, April 2026)?
anchor_measures: Day-one paternity leave and unpaid parental leave became statutory rights under the Employment Rights Act 2025 (in force 2026). Previously paternity required 26 weeks service; that qualifying period is removed.
source: gov.uk / Acas / business.gov.uk + Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order 2026; Emplo

question_asks: Do you provide dedicated paid fertility-treatment leave (separate from sick/annual leave)?
anchor_measures: CIPD 2023 Grade A all-UK: 22% of UK employers provide PAID time off to attend fertility appointments/manage treatment (34% public sector, 19% private). 27% provide flexibility of working hours (36% public, 24% private). 27% have a formal fertility-treatment POLICY (7% standalone + 20% within wider policy; 42% public, 24% private). 49% provide some kind of fertility support overall; 28% provide none. No statutory entitlement exists, so all provision is discretionary.
source: CIPD "Workplace support for employees experiencing fertility challenges, investigations or

anchor_measures: Statutory from Apr 2025 (up to 12wks); ENHANCED beyond stat is the seed's subject

question_asks: How many weeks of enhanced maternity and adoption pay are provided?
anchor_measures: Enhanced maternity/paternity/adoption/SPL: 60% large(250+); ~19% all UK-only (Fig28)
source: CIPD Reward Survey: Focus on Employee Benefits (Feb 2026, YouGov, n=1,059; large 250+ n=17

question_asks: Do you provide enhanced shared parental pay above statutory provision?
anchor_measures: Shared parental leave near-zero in practice: 85% of UK employers report NO new fathers/partners took up SPL in last 2 yrs; ~2% of eligible couples use SPL (downward trend vs 2016). Enhanced SPL pay correspondingly rare.
source: CIPD "Employer focus on working parents: parental leave and pay and childcare policies" (A

question_asks: Do you provide any paid parental leave beyond statutory unpaid parental leave?
anchor_measures: Statutory baseline (April 2026): SMP/SPP/ShPP £194.32/week (or 90% AWE if lower); SMP first 6 wks at 90% AWE. Paternity 2 weeks. Shared Parental Leave up to 50 wks shared. ERA 2025 made paternity & unpaid parental leave DAY-ONE rights. Neonatal Care Leave (up to 12 wks) live from April 2025.
source: gov.uk / Acas / business.gov.uk + Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order 2026; Emplo

question_asks: Do you provide paid carer's leave for employees with caring responsibilities?
anchor_measures: 52% of UK employers have a policy/procedure for employees to request carer's leave (75% public, 46% private, 59% voluntary; 67% large 250+ vs 33% SME). PAID carer's leave specifically offered by ~36% (RMS 2022) / 31-45% (flex 2025 support data). Carer's Leave Act 2023 gives 1 week UNPAID from Apr 2024; CIPD campaigns to make it paid.
source: CIPD "Flexible and hybrid working practices in 2025" (July 2025, ref 8909). YouGov, n=2,05

question_asks: Do you provide paid emergency or compassionate leave?
anchor_measures: Paid bereavement/compassionate leave is near-universal: 86% of UK employers offer it (up from 71% in 2018). Emergency/compassionate leave is among the most common paid-leave benefits, behind only 25+ days holiday (82%) in the discretionary-leave set.
source: CIPD "Reward Management Survey 2022: Focus on employee benefits" (April 2022, ref tcm18-10

question_asks: Do you provide paid study or exam leave?
anchor_measures: 70% of UK employers offer paid study leave (often grade/role-dependent rather than universal). Sits within a strong L&D benefits set: 92% offer training/career-development, 75% pay professional subscriptions, 75% conference attendance.
source: CIPD "Reward Management Survey 2022: Focus on employee benefits" (April 2022, ref tcm18-10

question_asks: Do you provide paid volunteering leave?
anchor_measures: 30% of UK employers offer paid time off for voluntary/community work. A minority, above-statutory practice (no statutory volunteering-leave right in UK).
source: CIPD "Reward Management Survey 2022: Focus on employee benefits" (April 2022, ref tcm18-10

question_asks: Do you offer sabbaticals or career breaks?
anchor_measures: Career breaks/sabbaticals are a minority benefit: 26% of UK employers offer UNPAID career breaks/sabbaticals; PAID sabbaticals are rarer still (low single-to-low-double-digit, among the least-common development benefits). Mostly grade/tenure-dependent.
source: CIPD "Reward Management Survey 2022: Focus on employee benefits" (April 2022, ref tcm18-10

question_asks: Do you provide specific paid leave related to menopause?
anchor_measures: CIPD 2023 Grade A all-UK (employer survey): 24% of UK organisations have a STAND-ALONE menopause policy + 16% include it within a wider policy (=~40% any policy), with a further 29% planning to introduce one. Among employers with menopause support: written policy 47%, support network 46%, OH/counselling 32%, planned flexible working 26%, temperature control 25%. NOTE forward-looking legislation: menopause action plans become MANDATORY 2027 for employers with 250+ staff (ERA 2025) - expect prevalence to rise sharply.

question_asks: How much annual leave do employees typically receive, excluding bank holidays?

question_asks: How are UK bank holidays treated for most employees?
anchor_measures: Statutory minimum holiday: 5.6 weeks (28 days for a 5-day week), and employers MAY include the 8 UK bank holidays within this 5.6 weeks. So "bank holidays on top of 28 days" is an enhancement, not a statutory requirement.

question_asks: Can employees buy or sell annual leave?

anchor_measures: For roles supporting remote work, 65% of UK employers require a minimum number of office days. Among those: 3 days/week most common (48%), 2 days (32%), 4 days (14%), 1 day (8%). By month: 5-10 days (26%) most common. 14% of hybrid employers plan to introduce/increase mandatory office days in next 12 months.

question_asks: Where are remote employees permitted to work from?
anchor_measures: Hybrid is the norm: 74% of UK employers operate hybrid working (42% formal policy, 23% informal). 65% require a minimum number of office days (up from 52% in 2023); most common is 3 days/week (48%), then 2 days (32%). Fully-homeworking (always WFH) offered by ~32%. Location specifics (e.g. overseas WFH) not separately quantified - most frame around office-attendance minimums.

Do you operate a skills- or capability-based pay framework?
In the last 12 months, have you enhanced benefits in response to cost-of-living or labour market pressures?
anchor_measures: Enhanced benefits in last 12m: 8% plan increase, 73% same, 6% decrease
source: CIPD Reward Survey: Focus on Employee Benefits (Feb 2026, YouGov, n=1,059; large 250+ n=17

question_asks: If an employee moves from office-based to remote working, how is their base pay typically treated?
anchor_measures: Moving office->remote rarely triggers a pay/benefit cut: only 4% of UK employers have reduced pay and/or benefits for predominantly-home-working staff; 64% have not and do not intend to; 11% plan to in future; 13% have instead provided a contribution toward home-working costs. CIPD flags equality/inclusion risks in differentiating hybrid vs office pay.
source: CIPD "Flexible and hybrid working practices in 2025" (July 2025, ref 8909). YouGov, n=2,05

question_asks: Which location is typically used to determine base pay for remote roles?
How are remote roles typically benchmarked for market pay?
Are pay decisions for remote roles reviewed to ensure consistency and fairness?
Do any pay premiums or discounts apply specifically to remote roles?
How are tips or service charges distributed to employees?
Does your organisation test whether top performers are paid above market median after pay reviews?
Are hourly workers paid if scheduled shifts are cancelled at short notice?
Do hourly-paid roles have guaranteed minimum contracted hours?
Which role levels participate in the tronc arrangement?
Which types of tips are included in the tronc arrangement?
What is your ideal market position for Pay?
anchor_measures: Most employers target market median across all levels (58-69%); slight upward skew at Exec/Director ('slightly above median' ~24%). Very few target below median.
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: Which sources do you use for external pay benchmarks?
Do you pay overtime?
Which allowances or premiums are currently paid?
For on-call arrangements, how is the allowance typically paid?
Is there a minimum call-out payment for call-outs (e.g., minimum hours paid)?
anchor_measures: 64% pay a call-out rate (Fixed-rate dominant)
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: Are allowances included in holiday pay calculations where relevant?
How do you apply for your salary review budget?
Which factors inform your annual pay budget?
Are pay increases differentiated by performance rating?
Over the past two years has your pay mix changed?
What is the lowest base hourly rate paid to any directly employed worker (excluding apprentices)?
source: gov.uk NLW/NMW rates (Apr 2026) cross-checked vs HR DataHub NLW planning decks

question_asks: On average, where does base salary sit versus market median by employee level?
Do employees receive customer tips or service charges as part of their total earnings?
Are tips or service charges distributed through a formal tronc arrangement?
On average, what is the total annual payment for each allowance type?
What is your max bonus % opportunity by level?
For deferred bonuses, what is the typical deferral period and vehicle (cash vs shares)?
How is the main bonus pool funded (top-down profit share vs bottom-up target-based)?
Which measures are used in your main bonus or incentive scheme?
anchor_measures: Most common bonus measures: Profitability 71%, Operational performance 68%, Individual performance 66%, Strategic goals 39%, Health & Safety 34%, Revenue 32%, Cost control 31% (n=62)
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: What is the typical individual/business % split of your main bonus scheme by level?
Is there a gatekeeper metric (e.g., profit, safety, compliance) that must be met for any payout?
Are bonuses deferred for any population?
Are malus provisions used (reduce unpaid awards) for misconduct/risk/compliance issues?
Are clawback provisions used (recover paid awards) in defined circumstances?
Are sign-on bonuses used for any roles?
anchor_measures: Sign-on bonuses: 15% of large UK employers actively using, 30% considering, 55% not using (=~45% use-or-considering) in 2022. CAVEAT: 2022 figure captured at the PEAK of the post-pandemic labour shortage - the report itself flags sign-on use 'at a level we have not seen before', so 15% is a CRISIS HIGH and likely sits above steady-state. Treat as an upper-bound read on prevalence.
source: HR DataHub Talent Attraction & Retention Survey, Oct 2022 (co-authored by David Whitfield)

question_asks: Are your incentives intended primarily to drive performance, retention, or cost control?
What proportion of employees are eligible for a bonus scheme?
anchor_measures: 12.2% of UK employees received a bonus in year to May 2025 (declining trend); small firms 20.4% vs large 7.8%. Receipt-rate proxy for eligibility - see denominator caveat.
source: Brightmine (formerly XpertHR) "Bonus Payments 2025" white paper, UK Compensation Planning

question_asks: In the last 12 months, what was the average payout as a percentage of maximum bonus potential?
anchor_measures: Typical achievement-level payout: 90-100% of target most common (26/43, 60%); next band 71-90% (~23%)
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: What is the target bonus opportunity by level (as % of base salary)?
Does your organisation operate any long-term incentive or equity plans for any levels?
anchor_measures: All-employee share plans (AESPs): only ~16% of UK employers operate them. Among performance-linked employers, group schemes incl. all-employee share plans 21-24%. Long-term/share-based incentives remain a minority, larger-employer practice.
source: CIPD "Pay, performance and transparency 2024" (Feb 2024, ref 8551, supported by ADP; resea

question_asks: Which types of long-term incentive plans are offered?
anchor_measures: Among UK employers operating group/long-term schemes: all-employee share plans ~16% overall; profit-sharing 19-32%; goal-sharing 24%; org-wide bonus 29-34%. Discretionary/individual LTIPs concentrate at senior levels.
source: CIPD "Pay, performance and transparency 2024" (Feb 2024, ref 8551, supported by ADP; resea

question_asks: Which levels are eligible for long-term incentives?
anchor_measures: Long-term/group incentives skew to senior staff: MPT (management/professional/technical) employees consistently more likely to be in group schemes than "other" staff across every scheme type (e.g. team bonus MPT 37% vs other 23%; profit-share 32% vs 19%). All-employee share plans ~16% overall.
source: CIPD "Pay, performance and transparency 2024" (Feb 2024, ref 8551, supported by ADP; resea

question_asks: Does your organisation operate sales commission or commission-based incentive plans?
Which commission plan structures are used?
What is the max annual LTI as a % of base pay by employee level?
What is the target annual LTI as a % of base pay by employee level?
What overtime multiplier is paid by shift type?
anchor_measures: Typical OT/unsocial-hours multipliers: weekday 1.5x, Saturday 1.5x, Sunday 2.0x, Bank Holiday 2.0x (day & night); night work escalates the rate
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: For hourly-paid roles, what pay multipliers apply by time band?
anchor_measures: Typical OT/unsocial-hours multipliers: weekday 1.5x, Saturday 1.5x, Sunday 2.0x, Bank Holiday 2.0x (day & night); night work escalates the rate
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: Which wellbeing/health data do you actively track (absence rates, PMI claims, wellbeing scores, EAP utilisation)?
anchor_measures: Wellbeing/health data tracked: not a clean single prevalence; absence-data analysis common

question_asks: Is there a documented financial wellbeing strategy beyond ad hoc provision?
anchor_measures: Financial wellbeing strategy/policy: 15% all orgs; 53% large have-or-plan
source: CIPD Reward Survey: Focus on Employee Benefits (Feb 2026, YouGov, n=1,059; large 250+ n=17

question_asks: What proportion of people managers have completed mental-health awareness training?
anchor_measures: Line managers trained for mental health: 29% (all orgs)
source: CIPD Health & Wellbeing at Work 2025 (Sept 2025, YouGov, n=1,101; large 250+ n=376; SME n=

question_asks: Do employees have access to occupational health, and what is the typical referral SLA?
anchor_measures: Occupational health services: 69% all; 86% large(250+); 39% SME
source: CIPD Health & Wellbeing at Work 2025 (Sept 2025, YouGov, n=1,101; large 250+ n=376; SME n=

question_asks: Is there an annual wellbeing budget per employee?
anchor_measures: Wellbeing budget exists: 54% large already have; 38% of those with FW policy overall; SMEs lower
source: CIPD Health & Wellbeing at Work 2025 (Sept 2025, YouGov, n=1,101; large 250+ n=376; SME n=

question_asks: Do you provide an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP)?
anchor_measures: EAP: 67% all employees; 78% large(250+); 39% SME (offered to all). NB HW Fig16=67% all
source: CIPD Health & Wellbeing at Work 2025 (Sept 2025, YouGov, n=1,101; large 250+ n=376; SME n=

question_asks: Do you offer a financial wellbeing programme?
anchor_measures: 64% have financial wellbeing policy/strategy
source: CIPD Reward Survey Feb 2026

question_asks: Which mental-health support provisions are offered?
anchor_measures: Mental-health support measures: counselling 43%, phased return 43%, EAP 41%, MHFA 40% (top measures, all orgs)
source: CIPD Health & Wellbeing at Work 2025 (Sept 2025, YouGov, n=1,101; large 250+ n=376; SME n=

question_asks: Do you offer health screening or health assessments?
anchor_measures: Health screening: 35% all; 47% large(250+); 23% SME (offered to all)
source: CIPD Health & Wellbeing at Work 2025 (Sept 2025, YouGov, n=1,101; large 250+ n=376; SME n=

question_asks: Is there a documented wellbeing strategy, and how often reviewed?
anchor_measures: Stand-alone wellbeing strategy: 57% all; rising to 75% at 1000+; 21% at 2-49
source: CIPD Health & Wellbeing at Work 2025 (Sept 2025, YouGov, n=1,101; large 250+ n=376; SME n=

question_asks: Is there an approximate annual recognition budget per employee?
anchor_measures: Annual gift-led recognition spend per employee (UK, directional): ~76% spend under GBP200/head/yr - 23.5% under GBP50, 52.9% GBP50-200, 9.4% GBP200-500, 3.5% GBP500-1,000; plus ~7% no programme / no formal budget. Per-head spend often LOWER at enterprise (budget spread thinner): ~30% of 1,000+ spend <GBP50/head.
source: Huggg "2026 UK Gifting & Recognition Benchmarks Report" (vendor survey via Typeform, field

question_asks: What is the typical value of an individual recognition award?
Are there limits on how frequently an individual can receive recognition awards?
Do you operate a long service award scheme?
anchor_measures: 76% operate a long-service award scheme
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: Which length-of-service milestones are recognised through long service awards?
How are long service awards typically provided?
anchor_measures: Most commonly Financial Award or Gift (some Combination financial+leave)
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: What is the typical value of a long service award at the main milestone?
Are recognition awards primarily monetary, experiential/voucher, or points-based?
anchor_measures: Recognition/gift currency (UK, directional): vouchers dominate at 43.5%; pick-and-send specific gift 32.9%; mix of approaches 27.1%; recipient choice 20%; CASH only 5.9% (and cash is perceived least effective for retention - 20% vs ~49% for gifts/vouchers). Mostly experiential/voucher rather than cash.
source: Huggg "2026 UK Gifting & Recognition Benchmarks Report" (vendor survey via Typeform, field

question_asks: Do you measure the impact of recognition on engagement or retention?
anchor_measures: Measuring recognition impact/ROI (UK, directional): only ~2% formally track ROI on gifting/recognition (clear metrics); 16.2% informal indicators; 56.4% do NOT track; 17.7% want to but do not know how. Belief-measurement gap: ~66% believe it aids retention, only ~2% can evidence it. (Secondary CIPD 2023: 89% offer some recognition but only 34% evaluate reward-strategy effectiveness.)
source: Huggg "2026 UK Gifting & Recognition Benchmarks Report" (vendor survey via Typeform, field

question_asks: Do you provide a personalised/flexible recognition budget to managers?
Do you operate a peer-to-peer recognition programme (points or social-recognition platform)?
anchor_measures: 66% have an employee recognition scheme in place (peer-to-peer subset not separately split)
source: lumi legacy platform (HR Data Hub intake), 2019-20

question_asks: Do job adverts typically include a salary or salary range?
anchor_measures: Job adverts include salary/range: ~40% always; 53% always-or-sometimes
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: Is there a formal policy describing pay progression within bands?
Does your organisation run disability pay gap analyses (or pay equity analyses by disability status) at least annually?
anchor_measures: 18% of UK employers ran disability pay gap analysis in year to Oct 2023 (27% of large 250+ employers had done so vs 9% of SMEs; 46% of large had NOT, 28% didn't know). No legal requirement currently.
source: CIPD "Pay, performance and transparency 2024" (Feb 2024, ref 8551, supported by ADP; resea

question_asks: Which pillars are explicitly included in your total rewards strategy?
Which best describes how promotion decisions are governed?
Do you have a documented annual reward/benefits communication plan (e.g., pay review comms, benefits comms, wellbeing comms)?
Does the organisation provide total reward statements to employees, and what proportion of employees access them annually?
anchor_measures: 34% of UK employers provide total reward statements to staff (37% private, 26% public, 17% voluntary; rises to 65% of 1,000+ employers; 52-53% of UK-with-overseas / overseas-with-UK operations).
source: CIPD "Pay, performance and transparency 2024" (Feb 2024, ref 8551, supported by ADP; resea

question_asks: Does your organisation have a documented total rewards strategy that integrates pay, benefits, wellbeing and career (or equivalent pillars)?
anchor_measures: Documented total-reward strategy: proxy via 77% have benefits objectives; 15% formal FW policy
source: CIPD Reward Survey: Focus on Employee Benefits (Feb 2026, YouGov, n=1,059; large 250+ n=17

question_asks: Does your organisation run ethnicity pay gap analyses at least annually?
anchor_measures: Ethnicity pay gap analysis: 18% carry out (10% share); 56% have not; main barrier company size
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: How does your organisation measure the effectiveness of reward/benefits communications?
Does the organisation run pay equity analyses across comparable roles (e.g., gender, ethnicity, disability), and how often?
When was your total rewards strategy last refreshed or formally reviewed?
Is there a standard minimum time-in-role or time-in-grade expectation before promotion (where applicable)?
Approximately what proportion of employees receive an off-cycle pay increase outside the annual review (excluding promotions) in a typical year?
Do you monitor promotion rates by protected characteristics to identify equity issues?
Do you have a published equality action plan, and which characteristics does it cover?
Do you use AI tools in pay decisions, and with what governance?
Do you run proactive equal-pay audits, and how often?
anchor_measures: Equal-pay audit in last 12m: 35% of employers
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: Can you group your workforce into categories of equal value (by skills, effort, responsibility and conditions)?
Do you include pay or a pay range in job adverts?
anchor_measures: Pay range in job adverts: ~40% always; 53% always-or-sometimes publish
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: Have you removed salary-history questions from your hiring process?
Are your employee benefits linked to defined objectives (e.g., retention, engagement, productivity) and reviewed against them?
anchor_measures: Benefits linked to defined objectives: 77% have objectives; 22% none
source: CIPD Reward Survey: Focus on Employee Benefits (Feb 2026, YouGov, n=1,059; large 250+ n=17

question_asks: Are you prepared for mandatory ethnicity and disability pay gap reporting for 250+ employers (Equality (Race and Disability) Bill)?
Where pay or pension/benefit terms are changed, do you avoid dismiss-and-rehire (fire-and-rehire restrictions, in force Jan 2027)?
Do you provide a personalised/flexible benefits allowance employees can allocate by choice?
anchor_measures: Broadstone 2023 (n≈200 UK employers): 61% do NOT offer flexible benefits; 39% do. Of those not offering, 67% have no plans to introduce. Of those offering, 71% use third-party platform. Drewberry 2026: benefits platform used by 25.2% of respondents for communication (different question, directionally consistent).
source: Broadstone UK Employee Benefits Survey, Nov 2023. ~200 UK employers. Grade B - broker clie

question_asks: Have you published (or do you plan to publish) a voluntary menopause action plan ahead of the 2027 mandate?
anchor_measures: Aon 2025: 57% of large UK employers now provide menopause training and information (up sharply from 25% in 2022). NOTE: this question asks about a published menopause POLICY specifically, which is narrower than Aon's "training and information" measure - treat 57% as an upper bound for menopause support, policy-publication rate lower.
source: Aon UK Benefits and Trends Survey 2025 (240+ UK employers, Spring 2025, Grade B, large-ske

question_asks: What is the size of your dedicated reward team relative to total FTE?
Where does final sign-off on the annual pay/reward decision sit (RemCo, HRD, Finance, CEO)?
How would you describe your UK pay-transparency position (adverts, internal ranges, published policy)?
anchor_measures: UK pay-transparency position: 53% publish ranges always/sometimes; only 16% publish both ranges+specifics
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: Do you apply reward parity / compliance checks for umbrella-company and agency workers (ahead of umbrella regulation)?
How prepared are you for the EU Pay Transparency Directive (2026)?
anchor_measures: EU Pay Transparency Directive prep: 44% unable to predict pay-transparency readiness; PTD adoption deadline June 2026
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: Does your organisation conduct gender pay gap analysis at least annually?
anchor_measures: Gender pay gap analysis: 44% carry out (30% always+14% sometimes share); mandatory 250+; public sector 52%; 79% of <250 had NOT
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: Are pay decisions reviewed for fairness before implementation (e.g., calibration, central review)?
Are exceptions to standard pay rules formally documented with justification?
Does your organisation review pay outcomes for compression and internal equity as part of the annual pay cycle?
Does your organisation review incentive outcomes by protected characteristic (where data is available and lawful)?
Does your organisation have a documented pay transparency approach or policy?
Do employees have access to pay ranges for their grade/role family?
anchor_measures: Employees have access to pay ranges for their grade: internal publication ~40% always; 32% never
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: Does your organisation publish pay ranges on job adverts?
anchor_measures: Publish salary ranges in external job adverts: ~40% always; 53% always-or-sometimes; 47% never/plans
source: CIPD Pay, Performance and Transparency 2024 (n=832; large 250+ 57%; SME 43%)

question_asks: Over the next 12-24 months, do you plan to expand external pay transparency?
Have you completed a pay transparency readiness assessment (e.g., audit of structures, data quality, communications)?
Are formal pay ranges defined for all permanent roles?
Are pay ranges linked to a documented job evaluation or grading framework?
How often are pay ranges reviewed and refreshed using market data?
Does your organisation use external pay benchmark data when setting pay ranges or offers?
Are allowances consolidated into base pay at any point (e.g., on promotion or contract change)?
Is progression eligibility linked to performance outcomes?
Does your organisation use promotion guidelines that specify recommended salary positioning on promotion?
What is pay progression based on outside of the annual review and promotions?
What is the typical maximum pay increase for promotions?
